description_long: This house features a traditional European half-timbered design with white plaster walls and dark wooden beams accentuating the structure. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with orange terracotta tiles, forming intersecting gables and a prominent overhang. The lower portion of the exterior is clad in gray stone, providing a sturdy base and visual contrast to the lighter upper walls. Windows are small and symmetrically arranged, with wooden frames, and the main entrance is sheltered by a wooden porch with railings. The house has two visible stories, and the overall form is L-shaped, creating a cozy, enclosed feel. Architectural details such as exposed beams, a raised entry, and a mix of materials evoke a rustic, medieval charm.
description: A two-story, L-shaped house with white plaster walls, dark timber framing, and a steep orange-tiled gable roof. Features include a stone base, wooden porch, and symmetrically arranged small windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a wooden porch, prominent gable roof, and visible timber framing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the L-shaped layout, intersecting gable roofs, and a secondary wooden door.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the stone base, symmetrical window arrangement, and the roof's overhang.
description_view_3: Back view emphasizing the long stretch of the house, stone foundation, and a covered side area.
attribute_entryway_design: Wooden door with a covered porch, timber posts, and simple railings
